objective: C3A-CP4 implements the next Checkpoint 3A child checkpoint: an executable media-artifacts probe that executes the local product/API path and evaluates runtime API-visible local/mock artifact evidence. Positive claims: C3A-CP4-HARNESS-001 dispatches the media-artifacts probe as an implemented executable pytest probe with shell=False and bounded/redacted failure output while preserving CP1 API E2E, CP2 output-correctness, and CP3 language-quality probes; C3A-CP4-MEDIA-001 creates approved synthetic projects through local API behavior, uploads approved synthetic knowledge, ingests/chunks/stores it, generates grounded walkthrough output, generates local/mock Stage 6 and Stage 7 artifact evidence, replays stored API-visible behavior, and verifies artifact MIME types, safe filenames, checksums, content binding, source-run/evaluation/citation/context binding, local/mock provider posture, no real media binary overclaim, and no cloned identity; C3A-CP4-NEG-001 proves docs/prose/static-snapshot, artifact-shape-only, and canned-success behavior inadequate. Later access/quota/retention, security/observability, performance, and real-browser E2E probes remain planned and do not complete Checkpoint 3A. Repository-ledger cleanup for PR #260 and issue #259 may be bundled because this is a substantive child checkpoint rather than a standalone status-only follow-up.
